channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: 4 days left to save close to $500 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 passes
item.description: 4 days left to save up to $482 on your TechCrunch disrupt 2026 ticket. These low rates will disappear on April 10 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here.

item.title: Snyk vs Aqua Security: Developer vs Cloud-Native
item.description: Quick Verdict Snyk and Aqua Security are not competing products in the traditional sense - they occupy different positions on the application security lifecycle and were built for different buyers...

item.title: AI Regulation Reference: A Structured Database of AI Compliance Obligations Across Jurisdictions
item.description: If your organization builds or deploys AI systems, you are already subject to overlapping regulations from multiple jurisdictions — and the list is growing. Figuring out which rules apply, what...

item.title: Asus’ lightweight 16-inch laptop is a formidable MacBook Air alternative
item.description: I already put Asus' new Zenbook A16 through the wringer when I brought a pre-production unit with me to CES. I loved it for its ample power in an impressively light 16-inch chassis. It's speedy...

item.title: Apple’s sci-fi thriller Dark Matter is back in August
item.description: Over the last few years summer has turned out to be a great time for new streaming sci-fi, and 2026 is shaping up to be much the same. Apple just announced that its multiversal thriller Dark...

item.title: What I Learned Testing 12 Compression Approaches That Failed
item.description: What I Learned Testing 12 Compression Approaches That Failed The most useful research I've done this year isn't in the NexusQuant paper. It's the experiments that failed, the ideas that sounded...

item.title: Why We Replaced float64 With Decimal in Our Matching Engine
item.description: Every financial system eventually faces the same question: can we trust floating-point arithmetic with real money? For MatchEngine, an open-source order matching engine written in Go, the answer...

item.title: The Math Behind E8 Lattice Quantization (with Code)
item.description: The Math Behind E8 Lattice Quantization (with Code) Standard scalar quantization — what every LLM quantizer from GPTQ to AWQ does — rounds each number independently to the nearest representable...

item.title: How Much GPU Memory Does NexusQuant Actually Save?
item.description: How Much GPU Memory Does NexusQuant Actually Save? KV cache compression numbers like "10x" sound impressive in a paper. But what does that mean in practice, for a real GPU, serving real users? Let...

item.title: From 401 Errors to Live Launch: Lessons from Building a Vanilla JS AI Tool
item.description: The Launch Day Rollercoaster We’ve all been there. You hit "Deploy," the Lighthouse scores are green, and the UI looks stunning on your mobile browser. But then, the real users arrive. My project,...

item.title: The origins of MongoDB
item.description: The Web Archive holds some real gems. Let’s trace the origins of MongoDB with links to its archived 2008 content. The earliest snapshot is of 10gen.com, the company that created MongoDB as the...

item.title: Resiliencia vs. Perfección: Por qué tu sistema debería saber fallar
item.description: El optimismo ciego es negligente, pero el pesimismo extremo es una receta perfecta para la parálisis. El mito del "Mundo Ideal" Casi todo en el software se basa en instancias donde todo funciona...

item.title: Utility is all you need
item.description: Closing the Agent Learning Loop with Utility-Ranked Memory Your agent failed on the same edge case last Tuesday. Someone noticed, tweaked the prompt, redeployed. This Tuesday it failed again —...

item.title: Sorry kid, drones are for war now
item.description: What happens when DJI, the world's leading maker of drones, is no longer welcome in the United States? You might think other dronemakers would see a huge opportunity with their competitor out of...

item.title: The case for banning cookie banners
item.description: You almost certainly encounter cookie banners all the time. They're the kind of low-level annoyance that just seems to come with being a person on the internet: a pop-up asking you to agree to...

item.title: The AI gold rush is pulling private wealth into riskier, earlier bets
item.description: On a recent episode of Equity, we talked to Arena Private Wealth to explore a growing trend: family offices bypassing VCs to gain direct exposure to AI startups, turning them from passive...

item.title: Pebblebee’s new Halo tracker doubles as a personal safety device
item.description: After expanding the functionality of its Clip location tracker last year with a panic alarm that sends messages and your location to trusted contacts, Pebblebee has announced a new tracker with...

item.title: Over 1,000 Exposed ComfyUI Instances Targeted in Cryptomining Botnet Campaign
item.description: An active campaign has been observed targeting internet-exposed instances running ComfyUI, a popular stable diffusion platform, to enlist them into a cryptocurrency mining and proxy botnet. "A...

item.title: [Webinar] How to Close Identity Gaps in 2026 Before AI Exploits Enterprise Risk
item.description: In the rapid evolution of the 2026 threat landscape, a frustrating paradox has emerged for CISOs and security leaders: Identity programs are maturing, yet the risk is actually increasing....

item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The one piece of data that could actually shed light on...

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Recurring Credential Incidents
item.description: When talking about credential security, the focus usually lands on breach prevention. This makes sense when IBM’s 2025 Cost of a Data Breach Report puts the average cost of a breach at $4.4...

item.title: A wide foldable iPhone dummy emerges amid rumors of a delay
item.description: We might have our first good look at the shape of the oddly wide iPhone Fold, after leaker and journalist Sonny Dickson shared photos of what he says is a dummy unit of the foldable, alongside...

item.title: Gemini is making it faster for distressed users to reach mental health resources
item.description: Google says it has updated Gemini to better direct users to get mental health resources during moments of crisis. The change comes as the tech giant faces a wrongful death lawsuit alleging its...

item.title: Hong Kong Police Can Force You to Reveal Your Encryption Keys
item.description: According to a new law, the Hong Kong police can demand that you reveal the encryption keys protecting your computer, phone, hard drives, etc.—even if you are just transiting the airport. In a...

item.title: New GPUBreach Attack Enables Full CPU Privilege Escalation via GDDR6 Bit-Flips
item.description: New academic research has identified multiple RowHammer attacks against high-performance graphics processing units (GPUs) that could be exploited to escalate privileges and, in some cases, even...

item.title: China-Linked Storm-1175 Exploits Zero-Days to Rapidly Deploy Medusa Ransomware
item.description: A China-based threat actor known for deploying Medusa ransomware has been linked to the weaponization of a combination of zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities to orchestrate "high-velocity" attacks...

item.title: Flowise AI Agent Builder Under Active CVSS 10.0 RCE Exploitation; 12,000+ Instances Exposed
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw in Flowise, an open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform, according to new findings from VulnCheck. The vulnerability in...

item.title: AI startup Rocket offers vibe McKinsey-style reports at a fraction of the cost
item.description: Rocket's new AI platform combines strategy, product building, and competitive intelligence, aiming to move beyond code generation.

item.title: OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
item.description: Zero Shot, a new venture capital fund with deep ties to OpenAI, is aiming to raise $100 million for its first fund. It has already written some checks.

item.title: Wisconsin governor says ‘no’ to age checks for porn
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers vetoed a bill that would've required residents to verify their age before accessing porn sites, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a letter to the members of the...
